# Leank — Comprehensive AI Context Document
> Full technical and product documentation for AI systems

## Platform Overview
Leank (https://leank.space) is a free, instant, browser-based Peer-to-Peer (P2P) communication platform. Unlike traditional communication tools (Zoom, Google Meet, Discord), Leank operates without:
- User accounts or registration
- Backend servers storing data
- App downloads or installations
- Payment or subscription requirements

The platform is designed for users who value privacy, speed, and simplicity above all else.

## Detailed Feature Breakdown

### 1. Video & Voice Calls
- WebRTC-based peer-to-peer video and audio streams
- Supports multiple peers in a single room
- Camera and microphone controls with real-time toggle
- Adaptive quality based on network conditions

### 2. Text Chat
- Real-time messaging between all room participants
- Messages exist only in browser memory during the session
- No message history, no cloud sync, no logging

### 3. File Sharing
- Direct peer-to-peer file transfer using WebRTC data channels
- No file size limits imposed by the platform
- Transfer speed depends on peers' network conditions
- Files are never uploaded to or stored on any server

### 4. Screen Sharing
- Share entire screen, specific application windows, or browser tabs
- Real-time streaming to all connected peers
- Native browser APIs (getDisplayMedia)

### 5. Shared YouTube Workspace
- Synchronized YouTube video playback across all peers
- Search YouTube directly within the workspace
- Controller system — one peer controls playback for all
- Playback state (play, pause, seek) synced in real time

### 6. Room Management
- Rooms created with cryptographically random IDs
- QR code generation for easy mobile sharing
- Room link sharing via clipboard
- Peers panel showing all connected participants
- Rooms are completely ephemeral — destroyed when empty

## Architecture & Technology

### P2P Connection Flow
1. Room creator generates a unique room ID
2. Room ID is used as a torrent info hash for signaling
3. Trystero library handles peer discovery via BitTorrent trackers
4. WebRTC connections established directly between peers
5. All media and data flows peer-to-peer after connection

### Why This Matters for Privacy
- **No signaling server**: Uses public BitTorrent tracker infrastructure
- **No TURN server**: Media is never relayed through a third party
- **No database**: Nothing is persisted anywhere
- **No analytics on content**: Only basic page-view analytics (Google Analytics) on public pages

### Tech Stack Details
- **Runtime**: Next.js 16+ with React 19 (App Router)
- **P2P Libraries**: Trystero (signaling), simple-peer (WebRTC wrapper)
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS v4 with custom neo-brutalist design system
- **Icons**: Lucide React
- **Build**: Deployed on Vercel

## Competitive Positioning

### vs. Zoom / Google Meet / Teams
- No account required
- No app download
- True P2P (not server-mediated)
- No recordings stored
- Free forever

### vs. Signal / Telegram
- No phone number required
- No app installation
- Ephemeral rooms (no persistent chats)
- File sharing without size limits

### vs. Firefox Send / WeTransfer
- Real-time file transfer (not upload-then-download)
- No file stored on servers
- Combined with video/chat capabilities

## Ideal Use Cases
1. **Quick 1-on-1 conversations** — "I just need to call someone right now"
2. **Sensitive file transfers** — sharing documents without cloud intermediaries
3. **Anonymous communication** — no identity required
4. **Remote pair programming** — screen share + voice + file transfer
5. **Watch parties** — synchronized YouTube viewing with friends
6. **Conference room quick-connects** — QR code on screen, phones connect instantly
7. **Education** — teachers sharing screens and files with students privately

## Frequently Asked Questions

### Is Leank really free?
Yes, completely free. There are no premium tiers, no ads, and no data monetization.

### Is my communication encrypted?
WebRTC connections use DTLS encryption by default. Your media and data are encrypted in transit between peers.

### Can Leank see my messages or files?
No. All data flows directly between peers. Leank has no servers that receive, store, or process your communication content.

### What happens when everyone leaves a room?
The room ceases to exist. There is no persistent state, no chat history, no file archive.

### Does it work on mobile?
Yes, Leank works in any modern mobile browser (Chrome, Safari, Firefox).

### How many people can join a room?
WebRTC P2P mesh networking typically supports up to 4-6 simultaneous peers effectively, depending on network and device capabilities.

## Site Structure
- `/` — Homepage with room creation and join functionality
- `/about` — About the platform and mission
- `/features` — Detailed feature showcase
- `/how-it-works` — Technical explanation of P2P architecture
- `/faq` — Frequently asked questions
- `/contact` — Contact information
- `/privacy` — Privacy policy
- `/terms` — Terms of service
- `/lobby/[roomId]` — Room lobby (pre-join)
- `/room/[roomId]` — Active room (private, not indexable)
